summary.notes: All portfolio and service slots filled from real client imagery. No stock photos were needed — CSM has a strong body of real infrastructure work (Torino metro, Italian highway toll gantries, prison fit-outs) that far exceeds what Unsplash could provide for a 50+ year metal fabrication workshop. The two dramatic welder shots ('hero' and 'hero-alt') are stock images the client themselves published on csm-rivaltaditorino.it — kept because they ARE the client's established brand imagery. Logo is a generic hammer icon served from the client CDN; flagged low-quality, Denis may want a proper wordmark.

logo.alt_text: Logo C.S.M. SNC di Bertacco F. & C.
logo.note: Generic hammer icon from client site — not a real logo. Consider replacement.

hero.alt_text: Saldatore al lavoro con scintille e fumo caldo, lavorazione metalli in officina C.S.M.
hero.note: Signature visual. Dramatic welder in red jacket with sparks — already published on the client site as their primary brand image.

hero-alt.alt_text: Carpentiere con maschera da saldatore blu durante lavorazione del ferro
hero-alt.note: Backup hero / atmospheric workshop shot for split-screen or secondary CTA section.

about-heritage.alt_text: Pensilina autostradale in acciaio su tangenziale realizzata da C.S.M. -- testimonianza di 50 anni di carpenteria metallica
about-heritage.note: Scale-of-work shot for the heritage/about section — speaks to 'oltre 50 anni di esperienza'.

unused_scraped_assets.reason: Generic stock photo of a blank metal door -- too weak/low-information to use in portfolio or services.

warnings: Logo is a generic hammer icon, not a real company mark. Denis may want to commission a wordmark or typeset C.S.M. in a strong serif/sans.
No team photos exist -- per brief, this is a workshop; frame the 'team' section around craft / machinery / 50+ year heritage instead of headshots.
hero.jpg and hero-alt.jpg are stock welder photos the client already uses on their own site. Ethically fine (they are the established brand visuals) but builder should not claim them as proprietary.
